graphifyNote: graphify was not run; Layer 2 taint-flow analysis fell back to per-file pattern matching with reduced precision. The source has zero HTML sinks, so the precision loss is immaterial here.

supplementalBaselineGaps.note: Hardening practices the prompt asked to grade that sit alongside but outside the four-layer per-check baseline. Reported here so they are visible without inflating the layer tallies.

items.evidence: No CodeQL or other SAST workflow in .github/workflows/ (only ci.yml, styleproof.yml, styleproof-approve.yml). Biome is a style/correctness linter, not a security SAST.
items.remediation: Add a github/codeql-action workflow on push + pull_request for javascript-typescript, or accept the gap as proportionate for a static demo.

items.evidence: No .github/dependabot.yml. Dependency freshness relies solely on `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` in ci.yml's security job, which reports but does not open update PRs.
items.remediation: Add .github/dependabot.yml with npm + github-actions ecosystems on a weekly schedule.

items.evidence: No SECURITY.md or .github/SECURITY.md. No documented vulnerability-disclosure path.
items.remediation: Add a SECURITY.md with a reporting contact and supported-versions note.

items.evidence: Linter is Biome; ESLint and the eslint-plugin-security / eslint-plugin-no-unsanitized / eslint-plugin-react-security family are absent, so `--with-scan` would find no recognised scanners.
items.remediation: Either accept Biome as the linter and rely on the static baseline, or add ESLint + eslint-plugin-no-unsanitized purely as a CI guard against future dangerouslySetInnerHTML regressions.

evidence: No localStorage/sessionStorage usage anywhere in src/. No auth library in dependencies. The app has no session concept.
checks.expectation: Long-lived session/auth tokens live in HttpOnly cookies, not localStorage/sessionStorage.
checks.gap: None — there are no tokens and no storage. The invariant cannot be violated.
checks.remediation: No action. Re-run this check if/when authentication is introduced.

evidence: No auth flow, no logout, no server.
checks.expectation: Logout calls a server endpoint, not just clearing local state.

evidence: No auth state of any kind; no network calls in src/.
checks.expectation: "Is the user logged in" resolves against a server source of truth on each load.

evidence: No OAuth/OIDC flow detected. Per baseline, skipped silently when no OAuth flow exists.
checks.expectation: Public browser clients use PKCE.

evidence: No OAuth flow detected.
checks.expectation: Every OAuth authorization request includes a validated CSRF-resistant state.

evidence: No OIDC flow detected. Skipped silently per baseline.
checks.expectation: OIDC flows include a validated nonce.

evidence: No router, no post-auth redirect, no ?next=/?returnTo= reflection. No router.push or window.location assignment from user input anywhere in src/.
checks.expectation: Post-auth redirect destinations are validated against an allowlist or restricted to relative paths.

evidence: No role/permission checks anywhere — there is no authorization model. This is the soft check; it reports partial by design because the audit cannot confirm a server-side check exists, and here none is needed.
checks.expectation: UI authorization checks are paired with server-enforced checks at the data layer.
checks.gap: Not a real gap for this demo — there is no authorization surface at all. Recorded as partial only to honour the soft-check vocabulary.
checks.remediation: No action while the app has no auth. If a backend and roles are added, ensure server-side enforcement accompanies any UI gating.

evidence: 0 occurrences of dangerouslySetInnerHTML in src/ or index.html. The only dynamic UI content — the <output> live region in src/features/threshold-counter/ThresholdCounter.tsx:25 — renders a fixed-vocabulary status label through React's auto-escaping JSX, not via any HTML sink.
checks.expectation: Every dangerouslySetInnerHTML consumes trusted/sanitized content.
checks.gap: None — the sink is never used.
checks.remediation: Keep it that way. If user-supplied HTML is ever rendered, wrap it in DOMPurify.

evidence: 0 occurrences of eval, new Function, Function(...), or string-form setTimeout/setInterval in src/.
checks.expectation: No dynamic code execution in source.

evidence: 0 occurrences of element.innerHTML/outerHTML assignment in src/. All DOM mutation goes through React.
checks.expectation: DOM mutations go through React, not raw innerHTML writes.

evidence: No user-controlled href/src/formAction in src/. The only <script src> (index.html:10) is a first-party module literal. No <a> tags with dynamic href exist.
checks.expectation: User-controlled URLs in href/src/formAction are protocol-validated (http/https/relative only).
checks.gap: None — no dynamic URL attributes exist.

evidence: No redirect call sites (router.push, window.location.assign, etc.) in src/. No query-parameter reflection into navigation.
checks.expectation: Redirects never pass user-controlled URLs through unchecked.

evidence: No Markdown or rich-text rendering library in the project. Skipped-silently per baseline; recorded as present because the invariant cannot be violated.
checks.expectation: Markdown/rich text renders with HTML disabled or sanitized.

evidence: No React.createElement with raw HTML strings, no custom JSX runtime that disables escaping, no third-party render-unsafe components. Standard @vitejs/plugin-react JSX runtime.
checks.expectation: React's escaping is never bypassed.

evidence: No deployment configuration detected (local-only). Per the baseline, projects with no detected deployment config report partial rather than violation for this check. The only http:// literal (playwright.config.ts:12) is a localhost dev/test default, not production source.

samples.note: http://localhost:5173 default — local dev/test only, not shipped

checks.expectation: HTTP redirects to HTTPS at the edge, or an HSTS header is present.
checks.gap: Neither exists, but there is no deployment layer to host them. The hosting platform (when chosen) must own HTTPS enforcement.
checks.remediation: When this template is deployed, enforce HTTPS at the platform/edge and add the headers below via that platform's config (vercel.json headers, netlify.toml/_headers, or Cloudflare _headers).

evidence: No HSTS header anywhere — no deployment config and no framework headers() to carry it.
checks.expectation: HSTS header with max-age >= 15768000; includeSubDomains and preload recommended.
checks.gap: Structural prerequisite (a header source) is absent.
checks.remediation: Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload at the deployment edge once a host is chosen.

evidence: No CSP via response header (no deployment config) and no <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"> in index.html (index.html:1-12). This is the single highest-leverage missing control: with no CSP, an injected script would face no defence-in-depth backstop.
checks.expectation: A CSP is set via response header or <meta http-equiv>.
checks.gap: No CSP exists in any form.
checks.remediation: Add a strict CSP. Cheapest in-repo option: a <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"> in index.html with default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'. Promote to a response header at the deployment edge for full coverage (meta cannot carry frame-ancestors or set HSTS).

evidence: No CSP exists, so there is nothing to evaluate for permissiveness. Recorded as not-applicable rather than present to avoid implying a policy was reviewed.
checks.expectation: CSP avoids unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval for script-src and avoids * sources.
checks.remediation: When adding the CSP above, do not include unsafe-inline or unsafe-eval in script-src.

evidence: No CSP frame-ancestors and no X-Frame-Options anywhere. The app can be framed by any origin — clickjacking is undefended.
checks.expectation: CSP frame-ancestors set, or X-Frame-Options DENY/SAMEORIGIN.
checks.remediation: Add frame-ancestors 'none' to the CSP (header form) or X-Frame-Options: DENY at the edge.

evidence: No X-Content-Type-Options header anywhere.
checks.expectation: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is configured.
checks.remediation: Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff at the deployment edge.

evidence: No Referrer-Policy header anywhere.
checks.expectation: A non-default Referrer-Policy (strict-origin-when-cross-origin or stricter).
checks.remediation: Add Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin at the deployment edge (or a <meta name="referrer"> tag as a stopgap).

evidence: No Permissions-Policy header anywhere.
checks.expectation: A Permissions-Policy restricts dangerous features (geolocation, camera, microphone, payment, USB).
checks.remediation: Add Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=(), payment=(), usb=() at the deployment edge.

evidence: No cookies are set by client code or any server adapter (no document.cookie, no auth library, no server). Nothing to flag.
checks.expectation: Auth/session cookies carry Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite.

evidence: No http:// asset or fetch URLs in production source. The single http:// literal (playwright.config.ts:12) is a localhost test default, excluded from the shipped bundle.
checks.expectation: Asset/fetch URLs use https:// or relative paths.

evidence: 0 secret-format regex matches (AWS access keys, Stripe keys, Google API keys, GitHub tokens, JWT, PEM blocks) across tracked source. CI also runs gitleaks (.github/workflows/ci.yml:118-121) as a defence-in-depth backstop on every push/PR.
checks.expectation: No API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys, or signing secrets as string literals.
checks.remediation: No action. The gitleaks job keeps this enforced going forward.

evidence: .gitignore:18-20 ignores .env and .env.* and re-includes !.env.example. git ls-files confirms only .env.example is tracked; it documents one non-secret CI knob (E2E_BASE_URL).
checks.expectation: .gitignore excludes .env, .env.local, .env.*.local.

evidence: No VITE_*, NEXT_PUBLIC_*, or PUBLIC_* variables exist. The only process.env reads (playwright.config.ts, e2e/styleproof.spec.ts) are CI/test-time (CI, E2E_BASE_URL, STYLEMAP_DIR) and never inlined into the client bundle. .env.example documents no secret-shaped public var.
checks.expectation: Public-prefixed env vars do not carry secret-shaped names/values.
checks.gap: None — no public-prefix vars are shipped.

evidence: No router and no link generation in src/, so no route/query parameters of any kind. Heuristic PII-by-parameter-name check has nothing to match. Reported partial per the soft-check vocabulary; no real exposure.
checks.expectation: URLs do not carry email/phone/gov-ID/token parameters.

evidence: No analytics library (gtag, posthog, mixpanel, segment, amplitude, heap) in dependencies or source. No event payloads exist.
checks.expectation: Analytics payloads exclude PII fields.

evidence: 0 external <script> tags. The single script (index.html:10) is a first-party Vite module; SRI does not apply to bundler-managed first-party entry points.
checks.expectation: Non-first-party external scripts carry an integrity attribute.
checks.remediation: No action. If a third-party CDN script is ever added, include an integrity hash.

evidence: 0 <iframe> elements in source.
checks.expectation: Third-party iframes use the sandbox attribute.

evidence: 0 window.addEventListener('message', …) handlers and 0 postMessage calls in source.
checks.expectation: message handlers validate event.origin before acting on event.data.

evidence: 0 <a target="_blank"> elements in source. No external links at all.
checks.expectation: target="_blank" links carry rel="noopener noreferrer".
checks.remediation: No action. Add rel="noopener noreferrer" to any future target="_blank" link.

evidence: 0 console.* calls in src/. No tokens, env values, or request-header objects are logged.
checks.expectation: No auth tokens, env values, or full header objects passed to console.* or loggers.

evidence: 0 MD5/SHA-1/createHash usages in source. No cryptographic code at all.
checks.expectation: No MD5/SHA-1 in security-relevant code paths.
